title: glob cache miss falls back to sieve_glob_to_regex at eval time
description: str_matches_glob has a defensive fallback that calls sieve_glob_to_regex + str_matches_regex_pat at eval time when the pattern is not in the cache. This fallback is documented as 'should not occur for validated scripts'. However there is a real trigger path: if variables substitution is enabled and the pattern argument to :matches is a variable reference (e.g. header :matches "Subject" "${pat}"), the expanded pattern at eval time will not be in the cache (the cache was built from the literal "${pat}" string). Since glob patterns can contain variable references in a variables-enabled script, the fallback is not just defensive — it is the normal code path for variable-pattern glob matching, and it recompiles the regex on every message. No test covers this case and no doc note mentions it.
